methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $4.5B - $29.3B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Anysphere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $29.3B

private.name: Wayve
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $8.6B

private.name: Cohere

private.name: Hugging Face
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

data.narrative:
Hive AI is a Series D-stage AI & Software company founded by Kevin Guo, currently valued at $2.0B post-money after raising $120M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private comparables (Anysphere, Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face) with valuations ranging from $4.5B to $29.3B, alongside 1 public comparable (Palantir).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $2.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier for Series D advancement, implies a valuation of $3.0B. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median of $7.8B, with a range of $4.5B to $29.3B. The peer range reflects material dispersion, driven in part by Anysphere's $29.3B valuation at the upper end.

Triangulating these two methods produces an implied range of $2.5B (low) to $9.0B (high), with a median of $5.4B. The current $2.0B valuation sits below the low bound of this range, suggesting potential upside to $5.4B–$9.0B depending on execution and market conditions. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.5x stage multiplier in the current environment and whether Anysphere represents a true comparable or an outlier driven by investor enthusiasm for frontier AI.
